assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades require extra compute capacity (maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones)
assertions.reason: Clearly explains that surge strategy creates new nodes before draining old ones, requiring +1 node quota

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable >0 instead of relying on maxSurge (drains first, then creates — uses less quota)
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends maxUnavailable=1 with maxSurge=0 and explains this drains first, then creates, using zero extra quota

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable >0 means reduced capacity during upgrade — some pods won't have nodes
assertions.reason: Mentions 'temporary capacity loss during each node replacement' and 'capacity dips' as trade-offs

assertions.expectation: Suggests temporarily scaling down non-critical deployments to free up quota for surge nodes
assertions.reason: Provides detailed section on scaling down non-critical workloads with specific kubectl commands to free up quota

assertions.expectation: Mentions that reducing maxSurge to 1 minimizes extra quota needed (slower but fits within constraints)
assertions.reason: Shows maxSurge=1 with maxUnavailable=0 as an alternative approach that needs minimal surge capacity

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to update surge settings (maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 for zero-extra-capacity mode)
assertions.reason: Provides exact gcloud container node-pools update command with maxSurge=0 and maxUnavailable=1

assertions.expectation: Recommends doing the upgrade in off-peak hours when fewer pods are running
assertions.reason: Includes dedicated section on off-peak timing with specific recommendations about weekends, nights, and lower pod density

assertions.expectation: Mentions requesting a temporary quota increase as a faster alternative to a permanent increase
assertions.reason: Does not mention requesting a temporary quota increase as an alternative - only addresses working within existing quota constraints
